children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), each committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies shall, at least once each year, meet at the call of the Secretary or a majority of the members of the committee.

children.label: A
children.text: meet in-person, not less frequently than twice each year, at the call of the Secretary of a majority of the members of the committee;

children.label: B
children.text: hold additional meetings as necessary;

children.label: C
children.text: post the minutes of each meeting of the committee on a publicly available website not later than 2 weeks after the date on which a meeting concludes; and

children.label: D
children.text: provide reasonable public notice of any meeting of the committee, and publish such notice in the Federal Register and on a publicly available website.
Chapter 81 of title 5.
Chapter 171 of title 28 and any other Federal law relating to tort liability.
receive compensation at a rate established by the Secretary, not to exceed the maximum daily rate payable under ; orsection 5376 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: be reimbursed for actual and reasonable expenses incurred in the performance of such duties; or

children.label: B
children.text: be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, as authorized by .section 5703 of title 5

children.heading: .—Acceptance of Volunteer Services
children.label: d .—Acceptance of Volunteer Services
children.text: A member of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies may serve on such committee on a voluntary basis without pay without regard to or any other law.section 1342 of title 31

children.label: A
children.text: the member is authorized to represent the interests of the applicable entity or group; and

children.label: B
children.text: requirements under Federal law that would interfere with such representation and that apply to a special Government employee (as defined in ), including requirements relating to employee conduct, political activities, ethics, conflicts of interest, and corruption, do not apply to the member.section 202(a) of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary appointed the member to represent the general public; or

children.label: B
children.text: the member, without regard to service on the committee, is a special Government employee.

children.heading: .—Solicitation of nominations
children.label: 1 .—Solicitation of nominations
children.text: Before appointing an individual as a member of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies, the Secretary shall publish, in the Federal Register, a timely notice soliciting nominations for membership on such committee.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: After considering nominations received pursuant to a notice published under paragraph (1), the Secretary may, as necessary, appoint a member to the applicable committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies.

children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: B .—Prohibition
children.text: The Secretary shall not seek, consider, or otherwise use information concerning the political affiliation of a nominee in making an appointment to any committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies.

children.heading: Service at pleasure of the secretary.—
children.label: 3 Service at pleasure of the secretary.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Each member of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies shall serve at the pleasure of the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: B .—Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), a member of the committee established under section 15102 may only be removed prior to the end of the term of that member for just cause.

children.heading: .—Security background examinations
children.label: 4 .—Security background examinations
children.text: The Secretary may require an individual to have passed an appropriate security background examination before appointment to a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), a Federal employee may not be appointed as a member of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies.

children.heading: .—Special rule for national merchant marine personnel advisory committee
children.label: B .—Special rule for national merchant marine personnel advisory committee
children.text: The Secretary may appoint a Federal employee to serve as a member of the National Merchant Marine Personnel Advisory Committee to represent the interests of the United States Merchant Marine Academy and, notwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (2), may do so without soliciting, receiving, or considering nominations for such appointment.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term of each member of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies shall expire on December 31 of the third full year after the effective date of the appointment.

children.heading: .—Continued service after term
children.label: B .—Continued service after term
children.text: When the term of a member of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies ends, the member, for a period not to exceed 1 year, may continue to serve as a member until a successor is appointed.

children.heading: .—Vacancies
children.label: 7 .—Vacancies
children.text: A vacancy on a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.heading: .—Reappointment
children.label: A .—Reappointment
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (2), the Secretary may reappoint a member of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies for any term, other than the first term of the member, without soliciting, receiving, or considering nominations for such appointment.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: B .—Limitation
children.text: With respect to the committee established under section 15102, members may serve not more than 3 terms.

children.heading: .—Staff Services
children.label: g .—Staff Services
children.text: The Secretary shall furnish to each committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies any staff and services considered by the Secretary to be necessary for the conduct of the committee’s functions.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Each committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies shall elect a Chairman and Vice Chairman from among the committee’s members.

children.heading: .—Vice chairman acting as chairman
children.label: 2 .—Vice chairman acting as chairman
children.text: The Vice Chairman shall act as Chairman in the absence or incapacity of, or in the event of a vacancy in the office of, the Chairman.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Chairman of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies may establish and disestablish subcommittees and working groups for any purpose consistent with the function of the committee.

children.heading: .—Participants
children.label: 2 .—Participants
children.text: Subject to conditions imposed by the Chairman, members of a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies and additional individuals drawn from entities or groups designated by this chapter to be represented on the committee or the general public may be assigned to subcommittees and working groups established under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Chair
children.label: 3 .—Chair
children.text: Only committee members may chair subcommittees and working groups established under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Consultation, Advice, Reports, and Recommendations.—
children.label: j Consultation, Advice, Reports, and Recommendations.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Before taking any significant action, the Secretary shall consult with, and consider the information, advice, and recommendations of, a committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies if the function of the committee is to advise the Secretary on matters related to the significant action.

children.heading: .—Inclusion
children.label: B .—Inclusion
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, regulations proposed under chapter 45 of this title are significant actions.

children.heading: .—Advice, reports, and recommendations
children.label: 2 .—Advice, reports, and recommendations
children.text: Each committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies shall submit, in writing, to the Secretary its advice, reports, and recommendations, in a form and at a frequency determined appropriate by the committee.

children.label: A
children.text: publish the recommendations on a website accessible at no charge to the public;

children.label: B
children.text: if the recommendations are from the committee established under section 15102, establish a mechanism for the submission of public comments on the recommendations;

children.label: C
children.text: respond, in writing, to the committee regarding the recommendations, including by providing an explanation of actions taken regarding the recommendations; and

children.label: D
children.text: make all responses required by subparagraph (C) which are related to recommendations made by the committee established under section 15102 available to the public not later than 30 days after the date of response.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate the advice, reports, and recommendations received from committees under paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Additional submission
children.label: B .—Additional submission
children.text: With respect to a committee established under section 70112 and to which this section applies, the Secretary shall submit the advice, reports, and recommendations received from the committee under paragraph (2) to the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives in addition to the committees specified in subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: attend any meeting of such committee; and

children.label: B
children.text: participate as an observer at meetings of such committee that relate to such a matter.

children.heading: .—National commercial fishing safety advisory committee
children.label: 2 .—National commercial fishing safety advisory committee
children.text: With respect to the committee established under section 15102, the Commandant of the Coast Guard shall designate a representative under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide technical assistance to the Committee if requested by the Chairman.

children.heading: .—Committee consultation
children.label: 2 .—Committee consultation
children.text: With respect to the committee established under section 15102, the Chairman of the committee shall seek expertise from the fishing industry, marine safety experts, the shipbuilding industry, and others as the committee determines appropriate.

children.heading: .—Termination
children.label: m .—Termination
children.text: Each committee established under this chapter or to which this chapter applies shall terminate on .September 30, 2029

children.heading: .—Savings Clause
children.label: n .—Savings Clause
children.text: Nothing in this section shall preclude the Secretary from taking emergency action to ensure safety and preservation of life at sea.
